steps.title: Identify the verb-object structure
steps.content: The word “unearthed” completes the verb “have.” What follows is the noun phrase “knives, horseshoes, wool tunics, and thousands of other artifacts,” which is the direct object of that verb.

steps.title: Apply the rule for verb-object punctuation
steps.content:
A verb must connect directly to its object without intervening punctuation.

The result is correct because it provides no punctuation between the verb and object.

The other choices all insert a mark (colon, comma, or em dash) that separates them.

That is the correct answer.
